locations.clue: These ancient royal tombs on the Giza plateau are the last surviving wonder of the ancient world — find them.

The world's largest hot desert, roughly the size of the United States, covers most of North Africa — pinpoint it.
The fourth-largest island in the world, it houses 90% of wildlife found nowhere else on Earth — where is it?
This 193-km waterway connects the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea, shortcutting the route around Africa — find it.
The world's deepest river and second-longest in Africa powers the Congo Basin rainforest — where does it flow?
At 1,470 m deep, this is the world's second-deepest lake, running along four African countries — where is it?
This semi-arid savanna spanning Botswana, Namibia and South Africa is home to meerkats and the San people — where is it?
One of the world's oldest deserts, its towering red dunes meet the Atlantic coast of Namibia — find it.

Drop a pin on the exact edge of the falls where the Zambezi River plunges into the gorge.

Find the flat summit of Table Mountain towering over Cape Town's waterfront.
The southernmost country on the African continent is known for its wine regions, savannas, and Cape of Good Hope — find it.
Table Mountain overlooks which South African city at the tip of the continent?

Find the ancient royal harbor of Alexandria, Egypt, where Cleopatra's underwater palace can still be seen.

Drop a pin at the Nile Delta where the world's longest river fans out into the Mediterranean.
The Nile flows north through Egypt before reaching which sea? Pinpoint the delta region.
Find the Uhuru Peak summit crater of Kilimanjaro in northern Tanzania.
Which East African country is home to Kilimanjaro, the Serengeti, and Zanzibar?

Find the Ripon Falls site in Uganda where the Nile begins its journey north.

Drop a pin in the heart of the world's largest hot desert, covering most of North Africa.

Find the Avenue of the Baobabs, one of Madagascar's most photographed landscapes.
The world's fourth-largest island, 90% of its wildlife is found nowhere else on Earth — where is it?
Madagascar lies off the coast of which African country to its west?
Find the southern entrance of the Suez Canal at the Gulf of Suez.

The world's largest hot desert, roughly the size of the United States, covers most of North Africa — pinpoint it.
Which landlocked West African country is home to Timbuktu and was once the heart of the Mali Empire?
This mountain range stretches across Morocco, Algeria, and Tunisia, separating the Sahara from the Mediterranean coast — pinpoint it.
This ancient fortified village on the former caravan route from the Sahara to Marrakesh has starred in Game of Thrones — find it.
The capital of Sudan sits at the confluence of the Blue and White Nile — where is it?
The world's second-largest mosque, rising 210 m above the Atlantic in Casablanca, was completed in 1993 — find it.
Which East African country, one of the oldest in the world and never colonized, is the birthplace of coffee?
The capital of Kenya sits at 1,795 m elevation and is one of Africa's most visited cities — where is it?
Which vast Tanzanian plain hosts the world's largest terrestrial mammal migration each year?
This 'Red City' at the foot of the Atlas Mountains is Morocco's most vibrant imperial city — find it.
The historic Stone Town of this Indian Ocean island was a major spice and slave trade hub — find it.
The capital and largest city of Botswana sits near the South African border — where is it?
This massive ancient Egyptian temple complex on the east bank of the Nile near Luxor was built over 2,000 years — find it.
On which plateau near Cairo do the three great pharaonic pyramids and the Sphinx stand?
Which Zimbabwean border town overlooks the thundering Victoria Falls on the Zambezi River?

Where does the Congo River reach the Atlantic Ocean in the DRC?
On which part of the East African Rift does Lake Tanganyika's Tanzanian shoreline sit?
In which central part of Botswana does the Kalahari's red sand stretch?
Where do the world's tallest sand dunes in Sossusvlei rise in the Namib Desert?
Can you pinpoint the Giza Plateau outside Cairo where the pyramids stand?
Find the exact lip of Victoria Falls on the Zimbabwean side.
Find Table Mountain National Park overlooking the Cape Peninsula.
Find al-Qarawiyyin mosque and university in the old medina of Fez.
locations.answer: Explorer Spot: Which city is home to the world's oldest university still in operation (founded 859 AD)

locations.clue: In which Moroccan city's ancient medina does the world's oldest university operate?
locations.answer: Regional Focus: Which city is home to the world's oldest university still in operation (founded 859 AD)

Drop a pin on Madagascar — the world's fourth-largest island off the east coast of Africa.
Where do the Atlas Mountains reach their highest point at Jebel Toubkal in Morocco?
The largest city in Africa and the capital of Egypt sits at the head of the Nile Delta — find it.
This semi-arid savanna spanning Botswana, Namibia and South Africa is home to meerkats and the San people — where is it?
The medina of which Moroccan city is dyed red with its famous clay walls and towers?
Which Moroccan mountain town is painted entirely in shades of blue?
Morocco's largest city and economic capital is also home to the world's tallest mosque — find it.

At 1,470 m deep, this is the world's second-deepest lake, running along four African countries — where is it?

Africa's fourth-longest river flows through six countries and plunges over Victoria Falls — trace it.
This vast inland river delta in Botswana is one of Africa's greatest wildlife sanctuaries — find it.

This island nation east of Madagascar is famous for the now-extinct dodo bird — where is it?

The capital of the Comoros Islands sits on Grande Comore island in the Indian Ocean — where is it?

The capital of Madagascar sits high on the central plateau of this island nation — where is it?

The capital and main port of the island nation of Mauritius — find it.
Morocco's capital is also home to the UNESCO-listed Chellah necropolis and Hassan Tower — where is it?
The capital of Namibia sits in the center of this vast southern African country — where is it?

South Africa's administrative capital, known as the Jacaranda City for its purple-bloomed trees — find it.
Tanzania's official capital, though Dar es Salaam remains the largest city — where is it?
The capital of Uganda is built across seven hills on the shores of Lake Victoria — find it.
The capital and largest city of Zambia sits on a railway junction in the African interior — find it.
The capital of Zimbabwe was called Salisbury until independence in 1982 — where is it today?
Which South African city sits between Table Mountain and the Atlantic Ocean near the Cape of Good Hope?
Which Tanzanian island off the East African coast is famous for its spice trade and Stone Town?
Which Egyptian city on the east bank of the Nile sits amid the largest open-air museum in the world?
The capital of Ghana on the Gulf of Guinea is the largest city in West Africa's Gold Coast — find it.
The largest city in Africa and Nigeria's economic capital sits on the Atlantic coast — where is it?

The westernmost capital city on the African continent juts into the Atlantic from Senegal — find it.
In which Tanzanian national park does Africa's highest peak tower above the clouds?
This legendary city at the edge of the Sahara was once the wealthiest city in the world — where is it?
Where in Tanzania does the Serengeti, home to the Great Migration, stretch across the plains?
Which Egyptian city sits near the High Dam that created Lake Nasser on the Nile?
Which African capital city has a national park right on its outskirts where lions roam?
